children.heading: Energy management system
children.label: 1 Energy management system
children.text: The term “energy management system” means a business management process based on standards of the American National Standards Institute that enables an organization to follow a systematic approach in achieving continual improvement of energy performance, including energy efficiency, security, use, and consumption.

children.label: A
children.text: receives funding from the Department;

children.label: B
children.text: provides an in-depth assessment of small- and medium-size manufacturer plant sites to evaluate the facilities, services, and manufacturing operations of the plant site; and

children.label: C
children.text: identifies opportunities for potential savings for small- and medium-size manufacturer plant sites from energy efficiency improvements, waste minimization, pollution prevention, and productivity improvement.

children.heading: Information and communication technology
children.label: 3 Information and communication technology
children.text: The term “information and communication technology” means any electronic system or equipment (including the content contained in the system or equipment) used to create, convert, communicate, or duplicate data or information, including computer hardware, firmware, software, communication protocols, networks, and data interfaces.

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: 4 Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given the term in .section 1001(a) of title 20

children.heading: North American Industry Classification System
children.label: 5 North American Industry Classification System
children.text: The term “North American Industry Classification System” means the standard used by Federal statistical agencies in classifying business establishments for the purpose of collecting, analyzing, and publishing statistical data relating to the business economy of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: classified in the North American Industry Classification System as any of sectors 31 through 33;

children.label: B
children.text: with gross annual sales of less than $100,000,000;

children.label: C
children.text: with fewer than 500 employees at the plant site; and

children.label: D
children.text: with annual energy bills totaling more than $100,000 and less than $3,500,000.

children.label: i
children.text: simulate manufacturing production lines;

children.label: ii
children.text: operate computer-controlled manufacturing equipment;

children.label: iii
children.text: monitor and communicate production line status; and

children.label: iv
children.text: manage and optimize energy productivity and cost throughout production;

children.label: B
children.text: model, simulate, and optimize the energy efficiency of a factory building;

children.label: C
children.text: monitor and optimize building energy performance;

children.label: D
children.text: model, simulate, and optimize the design of energy efficient and sustainable products, including the use of digital prototyping and additive manufacturing to enhance product design;

children.label: E
children.text: connect manufactured products in networks to monitor and optimize the performance of the networks, including automated network operations; and

children.label: F
children.text: digitally connect the supply chain network.
